description_long: This is a heavy-duty hammer with a large, cylindrical metal head featuring pointed spikes on both ends, giving it a menacing and robust appearance. The head is decorated with engraved zigzag patterns, adding a touch of ornamentation to its otherwise utilitarian design. The handle is long, straight, and appears to be made of wood, with a dark brown finish and subtle texturing for grip. At the base of the handle, there is a metallic cap adorned with a skull motif, enhancing the hammer's intimidating look. The overall construction suggests it is designed for powerful impacts, likely for demolition or ceremonial purposes rather than fine carpentry. The materials and design indicate durability and strength, suitable for urban environments where heavy-duty tools are required.
description: A large, spiked metal hammer with a decorated cylindrical head and a long wooden handle capped with a skull motif. The design is robust and intimidating, suitable for heavy-duty urban use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full cylindrical head with spikes on both sides and the engraved zigzag pattern clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the hammer head and the alignment of the spikes, with less visibility of the engraved pattern.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the opposite side of the head with spikes and the decorative pattern.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the head's thickness and spike arrangement, with minimal pattern detail visible.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle with a dark finish and subtle texturing, capped with a decorative metallic skull motif at the base.
attribute_head_type: Large cylindrical sledge-type head made of metal, featuring pointed spikes on both ends and engraved zigzag patterns along the sides.
